summary: Iteration 2 retest of Outstanding Documents Portal backend after fixes. Confirmed: (1) Both `id` and `_id` are now present in client + item + user payloads across all listed endpoints; full admin CRUD chain using returned `id` works (GET/PUT/DELETE no 500s). (2) New DELETE /api/admin/settings/smtp clears config (configured=false); submit with SMTP unconfigured returns fast JSON 503 with detail and item.status stays 'outstanding'. (3) All previously-passing flows still pass (auth login+cookie+Bearer, /auth/me, /auth/logout, search, password reset, CSV upload + replace, client counts/items, role 403s, submission 400 validation, admin reset item, SMTP password not echoed). Remaining HIGH bug: SMTP unreachable host (smtp.invalid.example.com) still returns Cloudflare HTML 502 because socket.gaierror from smtplib is not caught (only asyncio.TimeoutError appears to be handled). Item status is still correctly preserved as 'outstanding'.
passed: POST /api/admin/clients now returns BOTH `id` and `_id` with identical values
GET /api/admin/clients (list) returns `id` and `_id` per client + correct purchase/sales counts
PUT /api/admin/clients/{id} updates and returns both `id` and `_id`
POST /api/admin/clients/{id}/upload-csv with type=purchase form field: rows_imported=3, errors=[]; re-upload still 3 rows (replace works)
POST /api/admin/clients/{id}/reset-password: client can log in with new password
GET /api/client/items?type=purchase returns 3 items, each with `id` and `_id`
GET /api/auth/me returns both `id` and `_id` for the user
POST /api/auth/login + access_token cookie + Authorization: Bearer both accepted
DELETE /api/admin/settings/smtp (NEW endpoint) returns {ok:true} and subsequent GET shows configured=false
POST /api/client/items/{id}/submit with SMTP unconfigured -> HTTP 503 JSON {detail:'SMTP is not configured...'} in ~0s; item.status remains 'outstanding'
Submission validation: mode=no_photo + empty comment -> 400; invalid mode -> 400
POST /api/admin/items/{id}/reset puts item back to outstanding

failed.issue: When SMTP host is unresolvable (smtp.invalid.example.com), POST /api/client/items/{id}/submit returns a Cloudflare HTML `502 Bad gateway` page (content-type text/html), NOT the spec-required JSON 502. Backend traceback shows socket.gaierror raised from smtplib inside asyncio.to_thread; only asyncio.wait_for TimeoutError appears to be guarded — connection/DNS exceptions bubble up unhandled and crash the request, which the proxy then surfaces as 502 HTML. Note: item.status DOES correctly remain 'outstanding', so no state corruption.
failed.evidence: backend.err.log shows `socket.gaierror: [Errno -2] Name or service not known` from server.py:537 (smtplib.SMTP(...)) propagating through server.py:546 (asyncio.wait_for). Client receives HTML body starting with `<!DOCTYPE html>... preview.emergentagent.com | 502: Bad gateway` and Cloudflare branding. Elapsed ~0s (DNS fails immediately, well under the 15s timeout).

action_items: In backend/server.py around line 537-546, wrap the SMTP send in a broad try/except that catches smtplib.SMTPException, socket.gaierror, socket.timeout, OSError, ConnectionError, and asyncio.TimeoutError, and re-raise as HTTPException(status_code=502, detail='Unable to deliver email: <reason>') so clients get a JSON 502 instead of a proxy-level HTML 502. Keep item.status mutation AFTER successful send (already correct).
Optional: also catch the unconfigured-SMTP path inside the same try so callers get a consistent JSON error envelope.

success_rate.backend: ~95% (18/19 critical checks pass; 1 HIGH remaining on SMTP unreachable host)
